speaker: socky
dialog: Hello there valued reader. My name is Socky the Sock. Welcome to Michael's Playable Postcard Demonstration.

speaker: wolfgang
dialog: Why are you writing the text white on tan? You're going to make us blind!

speaker: socky
dialog: Wolfgang! Don't inturupt while I'm giving the introduction!

speaker: wolfgang
dialog: Sorry buddy!

speaker: socky
dialog: That was my friend Wolfgang. He can be a bit brutish. You can see our names in the top left corner of the screen while we converse.

speaker: wolfgang
dialog: As long as they can handle poor color contrast you mean...

speaker: socky
dialog: The foundation of the text adventure portion of Michael's game was taken from Nathan's dialoguing example.

speaker: dopey
dialog: The text adventure is only part of the game?! Color me intrigued!

speaker: socky
dialog: Be patient Dopey. First let's show off some additions made to the dialogue system; most important of which is options for player interation and branching dialogue!

speaker: dave
dialog: Show us! Show us!

speaker: socky
dialog: Ta-da!!! Just go ahead and click on the option that feels right to you. No pressure!

speaker: socky
dialog: DID YOU REALLY LIKE IT! :D

speaker: socky
dialog: I knew you would. I can tell you're a special player. I'm sure you'll beat the game when it releases.

speaker: socky
dialog: Let's move, on shall we?

speaker: socky
dialog: WHAT THE HECK DO YOU MEAN 'THIS IS IT'?!

speaker: carlos
dialog: Woooah... Maybe take it down a notch Socky. You don't want to leave a bad first impression with our new fan!

speaker: socky
dialog: You're right... I just don't know how to feel about having my game insulted...

speaker: socky
dialog: Let's move, on shall we?

speaker: socky
dialog: Now, you might've noticed that--

speaker: dave
dialog: WAIT! I wanna select a different option!!!

speaker: socky
dialog: Seriously...

speaker: dave
dialog: Please...

speaker: dave
dialog: YES! You won't regret this!

speaker: dave
dialog: dangit.

speaker: socky
dialog: As I was saying... you might've noticed that your spacebar input is paused when you're presented with choices.

speaker: socky
dialog: This way, you can't advance the dialogue and skip ahead to some spicy gossip you're not meant to hear!

speaker: doug
dialog: Take it from me, you're not missing anything.

speaker: socky
dialog: When you click a text option, the dialogue 'jumps' to a specific conversation. Typically, the end of these unique conversations will trigger a 'jump' back to a single converging conversation.

speaker: socky
dialog: It's not immediately obvious, but choices can also change how the game and story plays out in the long run. Specifically, choices can change a character's state in the other part of this game.

speaker: dopey
dialog: What's the 'other part' of the game? I'm so curious!

speaker: socky
dialog: I was just about to show that off! By advancing the text to its conclusion, you'll trigger the next part of the game: the on-the-ground point and click adventure section

speaker: socky
dialog: When you finish reading this, you'll flip to the other side of the postcard. When you do, simply click on which character's perspective you wish to view and you'll watch their story play out!
